# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nodejs-microservice
spec:
  replicas: <number_of_replicas> # Define the number of pods to be created. For example, if you want a deployment with three instances (3 Pods), set this value as '3'. Do not forget that Kubernetes will create one replica for each node in your cluster by default
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nodejs-microservice # This label is used to select the pod(s) associated with our microservices. It should be unique within a namespace and must correspond exactly between labels on Pod creation time, or not at all if there are no such matching Labels in this Deployment's template (i.e., Nodejs-microservice).
  template: # This section describes the pod(s) that will run with our microservices using Kubernetes YAML syntax and Docker images for each Pod to be created by default, if not specified otherwise hereafter in this file or on a node's image repository. The Nodejs-microservice label must match between these templates (i.e., the pod(s) associated with our microservices).
    metadata:  # This section describes how Kubernetes should create and manage Pods for us to run multiple instances of this service in parallel on different nodes within a cluster, by default it will be set up such that one replica is running per node. If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify the number here as '3'.
      labels:  # This section describes which Pod(s) should receive this particular instance of our microservice and how to handle requests from other services, by default it will be set up such that one label is associated with each node in your cluster. If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify the number here as '3'.
        app: nodejs-microservice # This must match between these templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template.  It is not necessary if there aren’t any matching Labels on the pod creation time or no such labels exist at all (i.e., none).
    spec: # This section describes how to run your Nodejs-microservice Pod(s) and what Docker image should be used for them, by default it will use a preconfigured one in our repository if not specified otherwise hereafter within this file or on the node's container registry.  If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
      containers: # This section describes how to run your Nodejs-microservice Pod(s) and what Docker image should be used for them, by default it will use a preconfigured one in our repository if not specified otherwise hereafter within this file or on the node's container registry.  If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
        - name: Nodejs-microservice # This must match between templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template.  It is not necessary if there aren’t any matching Labels on the pod creation time or no such labels exist at all (i.e., none).
          image: <docker_image> # This must match between templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template or on the node’s container registry.  If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
          ports: # This section describes how to expose your Nodejs-microservice Pod(s) and what port should be used for them, by default it will use a preconfigured one in our repository if not specified otherwise hereafter within this file or on the node's container registry.  If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
            - name: http # This must match between templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template.  It is not necessary if there aren’t any matching Labels on the pod creation time or no such labels exist at all (i.e., none).
              containerPort: <port> # This must match between templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template or on the node’s port registry (i.e., 80). If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
              protocol: TCP # This must match between templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template or on the node’s port registry (i.e., 80). If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
          imagePullPolicy: Always # This must match between templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template or on the node’s port registry (i.e., 80). If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
      volumes: # This section describes how to mount a persistent volume for your Nodejs-microservice Pod(s) and what type of storage should be used, by default it will use an existing one in our repository if not specified otherwise hereafter within this file or on the node's container registry.  If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
        - name: mongodb-storage # This must match between templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template.  It is not necessary if there aren’t any matching Labels on the pod creation time or no such labels exist at all (i.e., none).
          persistentVolumeClaim: # This section describes how to access your MongoDB database and what type of storage should be used, by default it will use an existing one in our repository if not specified otherwise hereafter within this file or on the node's container registry.  If you want more replicas (i.e., three pods), then specify these details as required below under 'spec:'.
            claimName: mongodb-pv # This must match between templates and Pod(s) to ensure they are correctly managed by Kubernetes, it should be unique within a namespace for each instance of our microservices in this Deployment's template.  It is not necessary if there aren’t any matching Labels on the pod creation time or no such labels exist at all (i.e., none).
